source.version: 6TSL05 (Practice Test #4 — digital SAT in nondigital format)

vocabulary_cues: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph/table to complete the text?
Which finding, if true, would most directly support/weaken X's hypothesis?
Which quotation from X most effectively illustrates the claim?
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: ... The student wants to X. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

subskills: Command of Evidence — Quantitative (data from graph/table)
Rhetorical Synthesis (notes → sentence with stated goal)
Boundaries (commas, semicolons, colons, dashes, periods, sentence boundaries)
Form, Structure, and Sense (subject-verb agreement, pronoun agreement, verb tense, modifier placement, possessives apostrophes, parallel structure, plural/possessive forms)

passage_genre_mix: literary excerpts (fiction, poetry — Glaspell, Whitman, Burnett, Pound, London, Mansfield, Shakespeare)
science research summaries (biology, planetary science, physics, geoscience, anthropology, archaeology)
social science / humanities (art, history, sociology, linguistics)
historical primary sources (Black writers — Grimké, Whitman, Wheatley)
passage_length_words: 25-150 words (single short paragraph or stanza); occasional Text 1 / Text 2 dual-passage at ~50 words each
graphic_inclusion_rate: ~10-15% of questions include bar graph, line graph, scatterplot, or data table
sample_stems: Vocab fill-in: 'OSIRIS-REx successfully _____ a sample of the surface' (A) attached (B) collected (C) followed (D) replaced — Answer: B
Main idea: 'Which choice best states the main idea of the text?' — passage from Burnett's Secret Garden
Text 1 / Text 2: 'Based on the texts, how would Graeber and Wengrow (Text 2) most likely respond to the conventional wisdom presented in Text 1?'
Function of sentence: 'Which choice best describes the function of the third sentence in the overall structure of the text?'
Quantitative command of evidence: 'Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph to complete the text?'
Quotation evidence: 'Which quotation from King Lear most effectively illustrates the claim?'

Standard English: 'professional authors who are paid to write other _____ but whose names never appear on book covers.' (A) people's stories (B) peoples story's (C) peoples stories (D) people's story's — Answer: A
Transition: 'Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?' — options include Instead, Likewise, Finally, Additionally
Rhetorical synthesis: 'The student wants to emphasize a difference between X and Y. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?'
